parser_note: Parser-stress scenario: a -C field-comparison rule and a near-many-F (10-field) AND'ed rule. All valid auditctl tokens (f3 section 2.3-2.4). Both narrowed -> MEDIUM additive. The point is parser robustness on -C and multi-F, not an extreme tier.

rules.note: execve narrowed by a uid!=euid field-comparison (privilege transition). -C maps to AUDIT_COMPARE_UID_TO_EUID (libaudit.c:1158). Narrowed => MEDIUM (~0.3x unrestricted execve, f3 section 6).
openat narrowed by 9 AND'ed -F fields (auid>=1000 excludes daemon noise). Heavily narrowed => low end of MEDIUM. 10 fields total, well under the 64-field limit (auditctl(8)).

rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: narrowed syscall ~0.3x unrestricted. execve-with-C-compare and a 9-field-narrowed openat both MEDIUM; openat lower due to heavy narrowing.
